name: Jewish/Catholic Apple Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Metzorba flour (also known as dot metzinger)
4 Macintosh apples, peeled, cored and sliced or cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Combine cinnamon and 5 tablespoons sugar, then sprinkle over the apples. Set aside.
3. In a large bowl, combine and beat the remaining ingredients except walnuts until smooth.
4. Stir in the chopped walnuts.
5. Pour half of the batter into a well-greased 10-inch tube pan.
6. Arrange half of the apple mixture on top of the batter.
7. Pour the remaining batter over the apples.
8. Arrange the rest of the apples on top of the batter.
9. Bake at 350°F for 1 1/2 hours.
10. Allow the cake to cool in an upright position before serving. The cake can be frozen for future use.
